title: DNS Resolution, Address Classes, Redirects, Proxies, and SSRF · Go Build Room
summary: A cooperative of small public-service publishers incrementally builds validated connection-time dial policy from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, graph, and report fixtures while retaining prior Go, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of validated connection-time dial policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Allowlist HTTP schemes and ports, reject userinfo and ambiguous host syntax, and prohibit unsupported protocols.
Use net/netip to classify all IPv4 and IPv6 answers including mapped, loopback, private, link-local, multicast, unspecified, and metadata ranges.
Bind admitted addresses to DialContext or a controlled egress proxy and verify each new connection and re-resolution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Scheme Port Userinfo
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 4952 during a keyboard-first accessible report review. Build deterministic Go evidence to block unsafe schemes, netip classes, mixed DNS, rebinding, proxy, metadata, and redirect destinations; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Scheme Port Userinfo.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for allowlist http schemes and ports, reject userinfo and ambiguous host syntax, and prohibit unsupported protocols. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Scheme Port Userinfo, keep Scheme Port Userinfo connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
